are a series of lawn bowls events to determine the National champions of England. The Championships were held from 19 August to 19 September 2021, at the Royal Leamington Spa Bowls Club in [Victoria Park, Leamington Spa](/wiki/Victoria_Park,_Leamington_Spa "Victoria Park, Leamington Spa"). They are organised by [Bowls England](/wiki/Bowls_England "Bowls England"), and are open to lawn bowlers who qualify via their County Championships. The 2021 finals were streamed live for the first time on [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The National Finals had been cancelled in 2020 due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

| * [v](/wiki/Template:BowlsEnglandNationalChampionships "Template:BowlsEnglandNationalChampionships") * [t](/w/index.php?title=Template_talk:BowlsEnglandNationalChampionships&action=edit&redlink=1 "Template talk:BowlsEnglandNationalChampionships (page does not exist)") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:BowlsEnglandNationalChampionships "Special:EditPage/Template:BowlsEnglandNationalChampionships") | | |

The **2021 Bowls England National Finals** are a series of lawn bowls events to determine the National champions of England. The Championships were held from 19 August to 19 September 2021, at the Royal Leamington Spa Bowls Club in [Victoria Park, Leamington Spa](/wiki/Victoria_Park,_Leamington_Spa "Victoria Park, Leamington Spa"). They are organised by [Bowls England](/wiki/Bowls_England "Bowls England"), and are open to lawn bowlers who qualify via their County Championships. The 2021 finals were streamed live for the first time on [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The National Finals had been cancelled in 2020 due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

[Sam Tolchard](/wiki/Sam_Tolchard "Sam Tolchard") won his sixth and seventh [men's national championship](/wiki/List_of_Bowls_England_men%27s_national_championships "List of Bowls England men's national championships") title after winning the men's singles and two wood singles.[[3]](#cite_note-KBC-3) His sister [Sophie Tolchard](/wiki/Sophie_Tolchard "Sophie Tolchard") won her eighth national title when winning the triples with [Harriet Stevens](/wiki/Harriet_Stevens "Harriet Stevens") and Emma Cooper.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[3]](#cite_note-KBC-3) [Stef Branfield](/wiki/Stef_Branfield "Stef Branfield") emulated Sam Tolchard in claiming two singles titles at the Championships, the bowler from the Clevedon club (made famous by the legendary [David Bryant](/wiki/David_Bryant_(bowls) "David Bryant (bowls)")) won the singles and followed it with the two wood singles a few days later. Branfield nearly made history by winning the champion of champion title as well but lost in the final to [Ellen Falkner](/wiki/Ellen_Falkner "Ellen Falkner") MBE.

In the women's pairs, sisters Katy Smith and Lucy Smith scored 4 points on the last end to overcome a 3 point deficit to defeat Margaret Smith & Sharmishta Patel.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
